title: Determine similar triangles: Angles
description: Given two triangles with some of their angle measures, determine whether the triangles are similar or not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which triangles are similar to $\triangle ABC$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
We can map a figure onto a **similar** figure using only rigid transformations and dilations. This means the figures must have exactly the same shapes, but may have different sizes.

For triangles in particular, two triangles are similar if all of their corresponding angles are [[☃ definition 1]].

Triangle $ABC$ has angle measures of $62^\circ$ and $52^\circ$. The third angle measure is therefore $180^\circ - 62^\circ - 52^\circ = 66^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Triangle $JKL$ has angle measures of $46^\circ$ and $104^\circ$. The third angle measure is $180^\circ - 46^\circ - 104^\circ = 30^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Therefore $\triangle JKL$ is not similar to $\triangle ABC$.

Triangle $MNP$ has angle measures of $73^\circ$ and $62^\circ$. The third angle measure is $180^\circ - 73^\circ - 62 ^\circ= 45^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Therefore $\triangle MNP$ is not similar to $\triangle ABC$.
In conclusion, *neither* of the triangles are similar to $\triangle ABC$.

options.alt: Triangle A B C. Angle B is sixty-two degrees. Angle C is fifty-two degrees.

choices.content:
![Triangle J K L. Angle J is forty-six degrees. Angle K is one hundred four degrees.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1ec94df6c51eb720f7a55dd404951c803feb3ec)  
$\triangle JKL$ only

![Triangle M N P. Angle M is sixty-two degrees. Angle N is seventy-three degrees.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/16800331013e27974c3b5e1f7e2dca6fbd8d5ce3)  
$\triangle MNP$ only
Both
Neither

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which triangles are similar to $\triangle ABC$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
We can map a figure onto a **similar** figure using only rigid transformations and dilations. This means the figures must have exactly the same shapes, but may have different sizes.

For triangles in particular, two triangles are similar if all of their corresponding angles are [[☃ definition 1]].

Triangle $ABC$ has angle measures of $115^\circ$ and $35^\circ$. The third angle measure is therefore $180^\circ - 115^\circ - 35^\circ = 30^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Triangle $DEF$ has angle measures of $35^\circ$ and $30^\circ$. The third angle measure is $180^\circ - 35^\circ - 30^\circ = 115^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Therefore $\triangle DEF$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$.

Triangle $JKL$ has angle measures of $68^\circ$ and $52^\circ$. The third angle measure is $180^\circ - 68^\circ - 52^\circ = 60^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Therefore $\triangle JKL$ is not similar to $\triangle ABC$.
In conclusion, only $\triangle DEF$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$.

options.alt: Triangle A B C. Angle A is one hundred fifteen degrees. Angle B is thirty-five degrees.

choices.content:
![Triangle D E F. Angle E is thirty-five degrees. Angle F is thirty degrees.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5dc9a2280322b7f2f01c15fc39483bb9697a8526)  
$\triangle DEF$ only

![Triangle J K L. Angle J is sixty-eight degrees. Angle L is fifty-two degrees.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56ee9d951fcf7f67767b6889560267c5fadb3498)  
$\triangle JKL$ only
Both
Neither

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which triangles are similar to $\triangle ABC$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
We can map a figure onto a **similar** figure using only rigid transformations and dilations. This means the figures must have exactly the same shapes, but may have different sizes.

For triangles in particular, two triangles are similar if all of their corresponding angles are [[☃ definition 3]].

Triangle $ABC$ has angle measures of $37^\circ$ and $47^\circ$. The third angle measure is therefore $180^\circ - 37^\circ - 47^\circ = 96^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Triangle $FED$ has angle measures of $103^\circ$ and $40^\circ$. The third angle measure is $180^\circ - 103^\circ - 40^\circ = 37^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Therefore $\triangle FED$ is not similar to $\triangle ABC$.

Triangle $HIG$ has angle measures of $68^\circ$ and $40^\circ$. The third angle measure is $180^\circ - 68^\circ - 40 ^\circ= 72^\circ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Therefore $\triangle HIG$ is not similar to $\triangle ABC$.
In conclusion, *neither* of the triangles is similar to $\triangle ABC$.
